zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Coppin Jr. Sherriff Devon於大約1496年出生在英國戴文波特萊其時，他的父親John Coffin23歲，母親Elizabeth Phillippa Hingston22歲。他於1530年之後與Wilmot Chudleigh在英國結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1511年被列為sheriff of devon。他去世於1555年12月24日在英國戴文阿爾文頓享年60歲，埋葬在英國戴文阿爾文頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Coppin Jr. Sherriff Devon于大约1496年出生在英国戴文波特萊其时，他的父亲John Coffin23岁，母亲Elizabeth Phillippa Hingston22岁。他于1530年之后与Wilmot Chudleigh在英国结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他于1511年被列为sheriff of devon。他去世于1555年12月24日在英国戴文阿爾文頓享年60岁，埋葬在英国戴文阿爾文頓。

nameMeanings.name: Coppin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Camille, Christophe.
English and French: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Copin, a pet form of Jacob .
English: variant of   Copping  .

variants.name: Coppins
meanings: English (Kent): variant of Coppin , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Americanized form of Flemish and Dutch   Coppens  .

variants.name: Copen
meanings: English: variant of   Coppin  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German   Koppen  .

variants.name: Copping
meanings: English:
nickname apparently from a derivative of Middle English cop ‘top, peak; (crown of the) head; tip (of the nose)’ (Old English copp).
variant of   Coppin  .

variants.name: Jacob
meanings: Jewish, English, Welsh, German, Portuguese, French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, Swedish, Danish, and Norwegian: derivative, via Latin Jacob(us), from the Hebrew personal name Ya‘aqob (Yaakov). In the Bible, this is the name of the crafty younger twin brother of Esau (Genesis 25:26), who took advantage of the latter's hunger and impetuousness to persuade him to part with his birthright ‘for a mess of potage’. The name is traditionally interpreted as coming from Hebrew akev ‘heel’: Jacob is said to have been born holding on to Esau's heel. In English usage the name Jacob is regarded as distinct from the name James , but they are of identical origin. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Yaqub , Yakub , Yacoub , or Yacob , Slovenian Jakob and Jakop, Czech and Slovak Jakub , and also their patronymics and other derivatives (see examples at Jacobs and Jacobson ). The name Jacob is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Chacko ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

nameMeanings.name: Jr.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Sang, Yong, Kyung, Wei, Young, Yuhe, Chen, Dong, Fang, Guoping, Hongbin.  Korean Chang, Dae, Min, Bok Nam, Byung, Do Young, Dong Sik, Hak, Jong Won, Jung Hwan, Jung Ok, Kwang Sun.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 紀, meaning ‘record’ or ‘era’ in Chinese: (i) from Ji (紀), the name of an ancient state (located in Shouguang in Shandong province) granted to descendants of the legendary Yan Di, the Yan Emperor. After the state was annexed by the state of Qi in 550  BC , the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of another Chinese surname, Shu (舒), during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ).

variants.name: Kit
meanings: Ukrainian and Rusyn: nickname from Ukrainian and Rusyn kit ‘cat’. As a Rusyn name it is also found in Poland.

Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 揭, meaning ‘raise, hoist’ in ancient Chinese: shortened form of the placename Jie Yang (揭陽) (located in present-day eastern Guangdong province), possibly borne by descendants of the governor of Jieyang originally called Shi Ding, also known as Jie Yang Ling (揭陽令) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC  - 25  AD ).
Chinese: Teochew, Hokkien, or Taiwanese form of the surname 吉, see   Ji   7.

variants.name: Kei
meanings: Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname of 紀, see   Ji   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 祁, see   Qi   2.
Japanese: written 荊 ‘briar’; the character can also be pronounced   Ibara  . Another version is written 慶 ‘rejoicing’. Both are rare in Japan.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Coppin Jr. Sherriff Devon was born about 1496, in Portledge, Devon, England, his father, John Coffin, was 23 and his mother, Elizabeth Phillippa Hingston, was 22. He married Wilmot Chudleigh after 1530, in England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. In 1511, at the age of 16, his occupation is listed as sheriff of devon. He died on 24 December 1555, in Alwington, Devon, England, at the age of 60, and was buried in Alwington, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Coppin Jr. Sherriff Devon est né environ 1496, à Portledge, Devon, Angleterre, son père, John Coffin, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Phillippa Hingston, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Wilmot Chudleigh après 1530, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. En 1511, à l’âge de 16 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of devon. Il est décédé le 24 décembre 1555, à Alwington, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Alwington, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Coppin Jr. Sherriff Devon um 1496 in Portledge, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Coffin 23 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Phillippa Hingston 22. Er hat nach 1530 in England Wilmot Chudleigh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. 1511, als er 16 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als sheriff of devon aufgeführt. Er starb am 24. Dezember 1555 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Alwington, Devon, England und wurde in Alwington, Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Coppin Jr. Sherriff Devon è nato il circa 1496 nel Portledge, Devon, England, il suo padre, John Coffin, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Phillippa Hingston, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Wilmot Chudleigh dopo il 1530, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Nel 1511, all'età di 16 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of devon. Morì il 24 dicembre 1555, nel Alwington, Devon, England, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a Alwington, Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Coppin Jr. Sherriff Devonが約1496年にイギリスデボンポルトレッジで生まれた時，父John Coffinは23歳，母Elizabeth Phillippa Hingstonは22歳であった。1530年以後にイギリスでWilmot Chudleighと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。1511年，16歳の時，職業はsheriff of devonと記録されている。彼は1555年12月24日にイギリスデボンアルウィントンで60歳で死去し，イギリスデボンアルウィントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Coppin Jr. Sherriff Devon(이)가 대략 1496년 영국 데폰 포틀레지에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Coffin(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Phillippa Hingston(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1530년 이후 영국에서 Wilmot Chudleigh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1511년, 16세의 나이로 그의 직업은 sheriff of devon이었다. 그는 1555년 12월 24일 영국 데폰 알윙턴에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 알윙턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Coppin Jr. Sherriff Devon nasceu aproximadamente 1496, em Portledge, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Coffin, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Phillippa Hingston, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Wilmot Chudleigh após 1530, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Em 1511, com 16 anos, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of devon. Ele faleceu em 24 de dezembro de 1555, em Alwington, Devon, Inglaterra, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em Alwington, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Coppin Jr. Sherriff Devon родился около 1496 г. в местности портледж, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени John Coffin было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Phillippa Hingston, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Wilmot Chudleigh после 1530 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. В 1511 году, в возрасте 16 лет,  он работал по профессии sheriff of devon. Он умер 24 декабря 1555 г. в местности алвингтон, девон, Англия в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности алвингтон, девон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Coppin Jr. Sherriff Devon nació aproximadamente 1496, en Portledge, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, John Coffin, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Phillippa Hingston, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Wilmot Chudleigh después de 1530, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. En 1511, a la edad de 16 años, su ocupación registrada era sheriff of devon. Murió el 24 de diciembre de 1555, en Alwington, Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en Alwington, Devon, Inglaterra.
